statement: A uniform slender rod of mass 4 kg and length 1.5 m is pinned at end A. The other end B rests on a frictionless surface (roller at B, vertical reaction only). The rod makes a 40° angle with the horizontal. A horizontal force P = 30 N is applied at B. Find the angular acceleration of the rod and the pin reaction at A at this instant. I_A = (1/3)·m·L². Use g = 9.81 m/s².
common_student_errors: Treating this as a statics problem (setting ΣM = 0) rather than using ΣM_A = I_A·α
Omitting the inertial term m·a_G when finding pin reactions with Newton's 2nd law
Mixing up sin and cos for weight component torques with the rod at an angle
